vide any clue to the correct time of birth.
V. According to some authorities the position of Prana
pada enables us to fix the time of birth. The concept of Prana
pada is based on the law of periodicity. Birth can take place
only at certain intervals of time. There is thus a correction
between the lagna and the degree of Pranapada. Parasara
observes that in rectifying the time ofbirth we have to con
sider Pranapada, and the position of the Moon and Gulika.
Convert the time of birth into Vighatis and multiply it by
two. Add the product to the Sun's longitude if the Sun is in a
movable (cardinal, Chara) sign. If the Sun is in a fixed sign
add the doubled Vighatis to the Sun's longitude and also add
240 degrees. If the Sun is in a dual (Common) sign add the
doubled Vighatis to the Sun's longitude and also add 120
degrees. Expunge multiples of360. The result gives the posi
tion of Pranapada.
For example, the birth Vighatis are 658. Multiplying this
